purpose: Validate domain manifest JSON documents against a strict schema and return actionable errors before registry ingestion or workflow composition.

endpoints.description: Validate one domain manifest document and return a pass or fail result with schema errors.

dependencies.purpose: Compile and execute JSON Schema validation for domain manifest documents

sidecar_specification.purpose: Schema validator for domain manifest JSON documents.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before loading manifests into a local registry or composing workflows from manifest metadata.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for runtime execution authorization, network schema negotiation, or arbitrary binary file validation.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Load a manifest JSON document. 2) Call /manifests/validate. 3) Ingest only when valid is true and error output is empty.

failure_modes.condition: Manifest omits required fields or contains invalid semantic version or capability structure
failure_modes.symptom: valid false with one or more schema error messages
failure_modes.recovery: Correct the manifest fields and re-run validation before registry load

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Compile the domain manifest JSON schema once, validate incoming objects with Ajv, and convert schema errors into a stable array of human-readable failure strings.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Manifest validation fails silently in some systems when JSON parsing and schema validation errors are mixed together; keep these error classes distinct.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Treat the validator as a reusable pure boundary that other registry loaders can call before accepting a manifest.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not recursively load directories or mutate registry state from the validator itself.
